source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A smooth quarter-circular track PQ is placed vertically, with its bottom end tangent to a horizontal conveyor belt. A small block $a$ with mass $m_{\mathrm{a}}=1\mathrm{kg}$ is released from rest at the highest point $P$ of the circular track. When it reaches the lowest point $Q$, it undergoes an elastic head-on collision with another small block $b$ of mass $m_{\mathrm{b}}=3\mathrm{kg}$ (the collision time is extremely short). It is known that the radius of the circular track is $R=0.8\mathrm{m}$, the length of the conveyor belt is $L=1.25m$, the conveyor belt rotates clockwise at a constant speed of $v=1\mathrm{m/s}$, the coefficient of kinetic friction between the small objects and the conveyor belt is $\mu=0.2$, and $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. What is the magnitude of the force exerted by the small block $a$ on the circular track at the instant before the collision?
2. What is the maximum height that the small block $a$ can reach after the collision?
3. What is the time required for the small block $b$ to move from the left end to the right end of the conveyor belt?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate notable visual reliance
The setup uses labeled geometric points and spatial relations (quarter-circular track, highest point P, lowest point Q, tangent connection to conveyor belt), which are typically aided by a diagram
However, the question text itself specifies the key geometry and parameters, so the image does not appear strictly indispensable
multi_step: This is a 3-part mechanics problem involving multiple linked stages: motion on a circular track, elastic collision, and motion on a conveyor belt with friction
Solving requires combining different principles such as energy conservation, centripetal-force/normal-force analysis, 1D elastic collision relations, and post-collision kinematics/dynamics
The provided metadata flags strong multi-solution potential: multi_solution_policy.mode=aggressive and should_push_multi_solution_agent=true
verifiability: The expected outputs are concrete numeric results with units (force, height, time), which are amenable to rule-based or symbolic checking
registry_passed=true and there are no asset_registry_record issues
The physics process is standard enough that intermediate steps can be independently checked by a downstream verifier

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和主图像均存在，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空
题目核心语义支撑充分，文本已包含关键几何与物理设定，样本可进入候选池
虽有 risk_flag=multi_part_answer_text，但不足以否定样本整体可用性，仅提示后续校验时需关注多问答案对齐

variants.rewritten_question_text:
A smooth quarter-circular track PQ is placed vertically, with its bottom end tangent to a horizontal conveyor belt. A small block $a$ with mass $m_{\mathrm{a}}=1\mathrm{kg}$ is released from rest at the highest point $P$ of the circular track. When it reaches the lowest point $Q$, it undergoes an elastic head-on collision with another small block $b$ of mass $m_{\mathrm{b}}=3\mathrm{kg}$ (the collision time is extremely short). It is known that the radius of the circular track is $R=0.8\mathrm{m}$, the length of the conveyor belt is $L=1.25\mathrm{m}$, the conveyor belt rotates clockwise at a constant speed of $v=1\mathrm{m/s}$, the coefficient of kinetic friction between the small objects and the conveyor belt is $\mu=0.2$, and $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. What is the magnitude of the force exerted by the small block $a$ on the circular track at the instant before the collision?
2. What is the maximum height that the small block $a$ can reach after the collision?
3. What is the time required for the small block $b$ to move from the left end to the right end of the conveyor belt?

open_ended_problem_variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
A smooth quarter-circular track PQ is placed vertically, with its bottom end tangent to a horizontal conveyor belt. A small block $a$ with mass $m_{\mathrm{a}}=1\mathrm{kg}$ is released from rest at the highest point $P$ of the circular track. When it reaches the lowest point $Q$, it undergoes an elastic head-on collision with another small block $b$ of mass $m_{\mathrm{b}}=3\mathrm{kg}$ (the collision time is extremely short). It is known that the radius of the circular track is $R=0.8\mathrm{m}$, the length of the conveyor belt is $L=1.25\mathrm{m}$, the conveyor belt rotates clockwise at a constant speed of $v=1\mathrm{m/s}$, the coefficient of kinetic friction between the small objects and the conveyor belt is $\mu=0.2$, and $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. What is the magnitude of the force exerted by the small block $a$ on the circular track at the instant before the collision?
2. What is the maximum height that the small block $a$ can reach after the collision?
3. What is the time required for the small block $b$ to move from the left end to the right end of the conveyor belt?
